activities_projects.title: Explain the Central Argument
activities_projects.objective: Understand the main argument in the poem
steps: Read the poem carefully
Identify the argument between the young man and the young woman
Write 4–5 sentences explaining their views
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the central conflict in the poem
activities_projects.evaluation: Clear explanation of both viewpoints (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Interpret the Phrase 'Ramparts at your ear'
steps: Locate the phrase in the poem
Explain what the poet means by it
Write 3–4 sentences about its significance

activities_projects.title: Hair and Identity
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the idea of physical beauty
steps: Note the importance of Anne Gregory’s hair
Explain why the young man mentions it
Write a short paragraph explaining its symbolic meaning

activities_projects.title: Role of the Old Religious Man
activities_projects.objective: Understand the final message of the poem
steps: Identify what the old religious man says
Explain its meaning
Write 4–5 sentences on its importance

activities_projects.title: Compare the Two Speakers
steps: List the views of the young man
List the views of the young woman
Write a short comparison

activities_projects.title: Theme Identification
steps: Identify the main theme of the poem
Support your answer with lines from the poem

activities_projects.title: Write a Short Analysis
steps: Think about the idea of true love
Write 5–6 sentences explaining how the poem presents this idea

activities_projects.title: Modern Relevance
steps: Think about how appearance affects people today
Write a paragraph connecting the poem with modern society
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies literary ideas to real life

activities_projects.title: Poetic Devices
steps: Find examples of imagery or metaphor in the poem
Explain their effect

activities_projects.title: Stanza Explanation
steps: Choose one stanza from the poem
Explain its meaning in your own words
